source.purpose: Protect the chapter-opening prompt contract that prevents mystery-forward openings from leaving the player unclear on who they are, why they are present, who is on-stage, and what pressure is actionable.

thread_lockdown_shortage.title: The sealed granary
thread_lockdown_shortage.resolutionCriteria: The Warden identifies who can lawfully open the stores before the settlement turns on itself.
thread_lockdown_shortage.failureMode: The lockdown converts shortage into public violence.
thread_lockdown_shortage.retrievalCue: The settlement is locked down and running out of stores.

npc_factor_marro.name: Factor Marro

agenda.pursuing: Keep the ledger closed until blame moves away from him.
agenda.currentMove: waits for the Warden to name who takes responsibility

npc_factor_marro.retrievalCue: Marro controls the ration ledger and wants responsibility displaced.

npc_speaker_ireth.name: Speaker Ireth

agenda.pursuing: Force the stores open before families run out.
agenda.currentMove: asks the Warden to make Marro answer in public

npc_speaker_ireth.retrievalCue: Ireth wants stores opened before locked-in families turn desperate.

frame.title: The Closed Stores
frame.premise: A locked-down settlement is running out of stores while two local offices push responsibility at each other.
frame.activePressure: The stores stay sealed until someone accepts lawful blame.
frame.centralTension: Procedure keeps order and starves families at the same time.
frame.objective: Find who can open the stores before the lockdown becomes violence.
frame.crucible: The Warden must decide whether to spend authority against procedure or public fear.

outcomeSpectrum.clean: The stores open without breaking Ireth's public standing.
outcomeSpectrum.costly: The stores open, but Marro records the Warden as the cause.
outcomeSpectrum.failure: The stores stay closed and the crowd turns on the speaker.
outcomeSpectrum.catastrophic: The lockdown becomes a riot no record can sanitize.

antagonistField.corePressure: Procedure can keep food sealed longer than fear can stay orderly.
antagonistField.escalationLogic: Every delayed answer moves blame from office to crowd.

openingSceneSpec.initialState: The Warden arrives because the stores are sealed and the lockdown crowd has started counting meals.
openingSceneSpec.firstPlayerFacing: Marro wants a lawful signature; Ireth wants the stores opened before families panic.
withheldPremiseFacts: Marro knows the seal order is no longer valid.

opening.sceneIntent: Marro and Ireth force the Warden to decide who carries the risk of opening sealed stores.
opening.openingPressure: The lockdown has made food a public fuse.
opening.chapterObjective: Find who can open the stores before the lockdown becomes violence.
opening.chapterCrucible: Authority spent against procedure may keep families alive and mark the Warden forever.
sceneWarnings: Establish the playable situation with enough context for the player to choose.
withheldPremiseFacts: Marro knows the seal order is no longer valid.

currentLocation.name: settlement ration vestibule
currentLocation.description: The Warden arrives because the stores are sealed and the lockdown crowd has started counting meals.

location.name: settlement ration vestibule
location.description: The Warden arrives because the stores are sealed and the lockdown crowd has started counting meals.

established: The Warden has been called because stores are sealed under lockdown.

userMessageContainsAll: who the PC is in this situation
why the PC is here now
what immediate pressure/problem the PC is dealing with
what each on-stage NPC wants, can block, can grant, or stands to lose
